queue_recommendation: Continue with Claim Discovery in smaller chunks because deterministic discovery packets, agent proof-routing judgment, and bundled-skill curation need separate acceptance review.

items.decision: Use a user-story index without visible U-numbering, ordered by user workflow and backed by AC-local specdown proof rather than separated Evidence sections.

Readiness can be treated as reviewed because its acceptance proof is deterministic: generated adapter setup, doctor ready/blocker states, missing specdown simulation, next bounded action, and bundled-skill orientation all pass through cheap specdown-visible Cautilus checks.

items.target: docs/specs/user/claim-discovery.spec.md#A user can discover declared behavior promises from selected repo docs.
items.decision: Does the story and first AC honestly show the selected docs boundary, claim discovery scope, and nonzero candidate discovery without pretending discovery proves behavior?

items.target: docs/specs/user/claim-discovery.spec.md#The claim packet is a proof plan, not a verdict.
items.decision: Does the spec make the non-verdict boundary visible enough for users and agents to avoid treating discovered claims as satisfied evidence?

items.target: docs/specs/user/claim-discovery.spec.md#The binary routes discovered claims into next-work buckets before fixture work starts.
items.decision: Does the visible output and artifact proof show that Cautilus routes discovered claims into human, agent, deterministic, eval-planning, and split/defer work before fixture work starts?

items.target: docs/specs/user/claim-discovery.spec.md#The bundled skill can curate the packet into a reviewable next-work map.
items.decision: Does the proof show enough Cautilus run or reviewed skill evidence that bundled-skill curation is not only deterministic packet discovery?
Does Behavior Evaluation explain why `eval` exists, name the `dev/repo`, `dev/skill`, `app/chat`, and `app/prompt` surfaces, and project latest evidence artifacts cheaply?
Does Bounded Optimization explain what improves, how intent and protected checks are preserved, and how on-demand proof feeds the spec report?
Does Host Ownership put adapter ownership, runtime wiring, credentials, prompts, models, and acceptance policy at the right product boundary?
Does Reviewable Artifacts stay cross-cutting while still carrying local specdown-visible evidence?
Does Evidence Gaps explain the difference between discovered claims, human review, and satisfied evidence without old proof-debt vocabulary?
